summary.intent: Fix moltis issue #657 by implementing native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) to replace shell-exec-based file operations, improving model quality, security, and observability.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 MVP fully implemented and committed across 7 commits on the inexpensive-primrose worktree branch. All 89+ fs unit/contract tests pass, gateway builds clean, clippy clean. Phase 3b (must-read-before-write invariant + re-read loop detection) partially implemented with files modified but not yet committed.

repo: AgentTool trait is defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 with name/description/parameters_schema/execute
ExecTool (crates/tools/src/exec.rs:194, 1.7K LOC) is the canonical template for new tools — already wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs
MCP tools are auto-namespaced as mcp__{server}__{tool}, so no direct collision with native tool names
ToolRegistry::register silently overwrites on duplicate keys — no warning was emitted before this session
Default policy profile has empty allow/deny (permissive), but the 'coding' profile only allowed ['exec','browser','memory'] — new fs tools had to be explicitly added
The 'coding' profile missing the new tool names would have silently dropped Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep for users on that profile
Gateway runs from systemd/launchd service working dir (not user project dir), so Glob/Grep defaulting to process cwd was a real usability bug
On macOS, /tmp is a symlink to /private/tmp — must canonicalize paths before recording in read tracker or must-read-before-write check silently fails
CheckpointManager is used only by AgentScopedMemoryWriter; Write/Edit/MultiEdit do not emit checkpoints, so fs mutations cannot be rolled back via checkpoint_restore
branch_session only forks conversation history (SessionStore messages), not filesystem state — both branches share the host fs by design
moltis_metrics pipeline exposes moltis_tool_executions_total and moltis_tool_execution_errors_total — new tools should use these rather than custom counters
just lint fails on pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA toolkit issue unrelated to application code — scoped clippy runs are needed
session_key_from helper reads session key from tool params context (same pattern as exec.rs)

code.finding: ExecTool — canonical template for new tools, shows ApprovalManager/SandboxRouter/policy wiring pattern
coding profile hardcoded to allow only ['exec','browser','memory'] — had to add six fs tool names
Tool registration site — register_fs_tools slotted in next to CalcTool

Central shared module: path canonicalization, binary detect (null-byte heuristic), cat-n line formatter, symlink rejection, byte cap (OUTPUT_BYTE_CAP), FsState for read tracker and must-read-before-write
Typed error taxonomy returned as Ok(Value) with kind field (not Err) so LLM can branch on not_found/permission_denied/too_large/binary/is_symlink — matches Claude Code behavior
EditOutcome enum enables CRLF-tolerant recovery: on no-match, retries with collapsed-whitespace normalization and includes hint in error payload
register_fs_tools accepts Option<FsState> for session-scoped read tracking; contract tests live here

workflow: Three reference repos provided complementary value: claw-code = tool taxonomy only; openclaw = edge-case semantics (adaptive paging, edit recovery, Claude param compat); hermes = agent-loop concerns (re-read tracking, path policy, backend abstraction)
Matching Claude Code parameter names exactly (file_path, old_string, new_string, replace_all, etc.) from day one avoids the normalization cost openclaw paid retrofitting Claude param compat
Splitting large beads into sub-beads (8o1 → q8d/ixv/joh) enables incremental close and cleaner commit history
Discovered-from beads filed immediately during audit capture gaps without polluting the main work stream
Running cargo check -p moltis-tools before the full workspace avoids CUDA build errors from unrelated crates
Typed error payloads as Ok(Value) with kind field (not Err strings) is the correct pattern for LLM-facing tools so the model can make decisions based on error type
Atomic writes via tempfile::NamedTempFile::persist prevent partial-write corruption
Canonicalizing paths at tool boundary (not just at policy check) prevents symlink-escape attacks and fixes macOS /tmp vs /private/tmp mismatches

friction: just lint fails workspace-wide due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA toolkit build error — had to use scoped clippy runs per crate
bd dolt pull failed at session start ('remote origin not found') — beads may be out of sync with shared store
macOS /tmp → /private/tmp symlink caused must-read-before-write check to silently fail until canonicalization was applied in ReadTool before recording to FsState
metrics API surface was unclear — had to investigate process.rs to find the right counter invocation pattern before stripping custom metrics and using existing pipeline
The infer crate was added to workspace deps then had to be removed after switching to null-byte heuristic — wasted a Cargo.toml round-trip
Multiple clippy passes needed (collapsible_if, needless_range_loop, unused qualifications from workspace deny lints) before clean build
open_items: moltis-sug — phase 2: sandbox bridge (SandboxFsBridge trait + shell/tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox; native Read/Glob/Grep bridge)
moltis-joh — phase 3c: adaptive paging (context-window-coupled page size, port openclaw formula: CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE=4, MAX_ADAPTIVE_READ_PAGES=8)
moltis-6co — phase 4: [tools.fs] config section (allow_paths, deny_paths, workspace_only, max_read_bytes, binary_policy, respect_gitignore, follow_symlinks, must_read_before_write)
moltis-6kx — phase 4 follow-up: Write/Edit/MultiEdit should emit CheckpointManager events so fs mutations can be rolled back via checkpoint_restore
moltis-ixv phase 3b code changes are in crates/tools/src/fs/write.rs (and others) but NOT yet committed — session ended mid-commit
Nothing pushed yet — all 7 commits are local on inexpensive-primrose worktree branch, not merged to main
moltis-tqh Read total output byte cap is implemented but the configurable max_read_bytes via [tools.fs] config is deferred to phase 4
WASM-isolated fs tools explicitly out of scope — future phase 5 if pursued
NotebookEdit tool not in issue #657 scope — explicitly deferred
Streaming reads for large files: current impl loads full file into memory even for offset+limit reads — deferred to phase 3 sub-task
No benchmark/perf gate against rg subprocess baseline — deferred
Windows path handling explicitly not supported but not documented — platform scope should be documented in phase 4 docs
